pi_items.source: Verified from countries_base.gvsproj/state.xml where the default feature layer is named ne_110m_admin_0_countries, matching the task instructions.
Verified via the export logic in export_result.sh which specifically collects the shapefile from this path to evaluate the task.

Downloaded the actual shapefile from Natural Earth (https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip) and analyzed with pyshp: the file contains exactly 177 features. 150 is a valid lower bound.

Downloaded the actual shapefile from Natural Earth (https://naciscdn.org/naturalearth/110m/cultural/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.zip) and analyzed with pyshp: the file contains exactly 177 features. 200 is a valid upper bound.

The ESRI Shapefile specification defines 1 as Point, 11 as PointZ, and 21 as PointM. Since a centroid operation inherently returns point geometries, these are exactly the correct expected shape types.

privileged_info_summary: The input dataset is the real Natural Earth 1:110m Cultural Countries dataset, which contains exactly 177 polygon features. A correct centroid output shapefile must therefore have exactly 177 point features (validating the 150-200 boundary range check) and shape types of 1, 11, or 21 (all points). The expected input layer 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' and output path '/home/ga/gvsig_data/exports/country_centroids.shp' have been correctly set in the project files and evaluation scripts.
